title: Restio calcicola
taxonomy: Restio calcicola is a species of plant in the family Restionaceae. It is a type of restio, a genus of flowering plants that is native to southern Africa. The family Restionaceae is a group of monocotyledonous flowering plants commonly known as restio rushes. Restionaceae belongs to the order Poales, which includes other monocotyledonous flowering plants such as grasses and sedges. The classification of Restio calcicola reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in its family and order.

intro: Restio calcicola, also known as "Calcicole Restio", is a type of restio grass, a genus of plants in the family Restionaceae, commonly found in the fynbos vegetation of South Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and allergies, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Restio calcicola include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, Restio calcicola is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are commonly used to treat respiratory conditions, and it is also used in the production of herbal capsules and tinctures.
